classObjective: Determine whether a regular polygon will tessellate the plane by examining its interior angle measure.
workedExamples: 13-5, Example 1 — Determine Whether Regular Polygons Tessellate the Plane
13-5, Example 2 — Determine Whether Semiregular Uniform Tessellations Exist
warmUp: What is the interior angle of a regular hexagon?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: interior angle formula (180(n-2)/n); 360° ÷ interior angle must be whole number; triangle, square, hexagon tessellations
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice determining tessellation capability from interior angles
independentPractice: Practice problems determining whether regular polygons tessellate and analyzing tessellation patterns
assessment: Exit ticket: determine whether a regular pentagon tessellates the plane and explain why or why not
capReflection: Why can any triangle or quadrilateral tessellate the plane, but only certain regular polygons can?
